summary: Current HEAD keeps the Story, Architecture, 17-clause Spec, implementation invariants, CLI surfaces, and regression evidence consistent; no role-blocking contradiction was found.
inspection_summary: Compared all ten Story acceptance criteria with the Architecture decisions and every Spec clause, traced those contracts into guarded-run-session/CLI/Git-identity code and named tests, inspected current-head evidence, and reran the focused 40-test suite successfully.
inspection_inputs: node --test test/guarded-run-session.test.js (40 passed, 0 failed at HEAD 41a6cb076c3ba03096c8e27545c246211158a9c0)
judgment_delta: Initial concern that the late stop-reason/state-shape fixes might leave Story, Architecture, or Spec text stale -> final conclusion pass because GRS-S-2, INV-001, and INV-002 now consistently distinguish new recoverable transitions from readable historical null reasons and bind malformed canonical/predecessor nullable fields to nonmutating invalid_state behavior.

Initial regression concern that additive guarded routes might alter legacy execute behavior -> final conclusion pass because C-001 preserves the run-id-free status route, the CLI dispatch keeps start/status/next/reconcile/merge on their legacy paths, the focused suite proves the compatibility surface, and the current-head full-suite artifact records 1130/1130 passing.

Initial path-surface concern that authority, fallback, migration, implicit selection, repair, and human/JSON outputs might cover only the happy path -> final conclusion pass because the Architecture and clauses C-005/C-006/C-007, S-001/S-002/S-005-S-009, and INV-004 map to concrete pre-fix-sensitive tests for repository, managed, source_fallback, stale/missing/corrupt/future-schema, mirror failure/repair, traversal, lock, restart, and legacy output surfaces.
